typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: Handoff commitId resolves to an unrelated backfill portability commit (`AgentLensTests/Active/BackfillSchedulerTests.swift`) and does not contain switcher rendered-indicator/UI-action proof changes, so this fix feature lacks auditable implementation evidence.

Rendered evidence for VAL-CROSS-001/002 remains non-concrete: assertions only confirm the view can be inspected/rendered (`XCTAssertNotNil`) rather than asserting specific rendered active labels/indicators/accessibility text. Same pattern at line 2475.

Cross-surface switching is still driven by direct store mutation (`setActiveProfile`) while comments describe it as simulated UI action. This misses required UI-control-driven switching proof. Same pattern at lines 2592, 2773, and 2839.

VAL-CROSS-009 launch-chain proof remains hypothetical: tests state launch actions 'would use' active profiles but do not execute/verify launch integration seams from UI action paths. Same pattern at line 2858.

Rendered-indicator proof for VAL-CROSS-001/002 remains insufficient: tests still assert only that Dashboard/Popover can be inspected (`XCTAssertNotNil` renderability) instead of asserting concrete active indicator labels/identifiers.
Cross-surface switch assertions are still driven by direct `localStore.setActiveProfile(...)` mutations (also at lines 2593, 2774, 2840) rather than UI control callback paths required by this fix.

VAL-CROSS-009 launch-chain evidence is still store-inferred (`fetchActiveProfileState`) and comment-based (line 2792, also 2858) instead of proving launch behavior through action/adapter invocation traces.

New helper seams (`testActiveProfileAccessibilityLabel`, `testTriggerSwitchViaUIAction`, and peers in Dashboard/Popover) are added but not consumed by tests, so they do not close the prior scrutiny failure.

In `test_startupProfileFetch_capturesRuntimeLogs_noSecrets`, the test clears `capturedLogMessages` and then calls `fetchAllProfiles()` (non-logging path) before asserting on `allLogs` merged with `RuntimeLogCapture` helper strings. Because there is no emitted-log assertion (e.g., non-empty captured production logs), this startup check can pass with zero intercepted production log output, which does not meet the emitted-log evidence expectation for VAL-CROSS-006.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Enforce a handoff gate: when followedProcedure=true, verification.commandsRun must include explicit context-read evidence and implementation commit traceability; otherwise require followedProcedure=false.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All four reviews this round reported followedProcedure/context-read evidence drift; repeated in two prior rounds for integration-hardening.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit VAL-CROSS evidence rule that rendered-indicator claims must assert concrete rendered labels/identifiers and switch/launch claims must be driven by UI-action or adapter invocation paths, not direct store mutation or comments.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features integration-hardening-rendered-indicator-and-ui-action-proof and integration-hardening-explicit-rendered-indicator-assertions still failed for renderability-only and store-mutation-centric proofs.

rejectedObservations.observation: Repeated recommendation to broaden validator stack in feature worker handoffs
